I have A♣8♦ in middle position with a deep ~109bb stack. This is a standard open from MP — it's an ace with decent playability that I want to take the initiative with and avoid getting multiway. Raise sizing: open to ~3bb (300) to isolate and build the pot when I likely have the best high-card, and to price out some speculative hands behind. I recognize aggressive players are behind (Button and SB) who may 3-bet; plan is to fold to a large 3-bet, but to call a small single cold 3-bet in position and play postflop (c-bet A-high and dry boards for value, pot-control and fold vs heavy resistance on wet/connected boards).

Minni opens to 3bb from middle position and we’re in the cutoff with T4 offsuit—an unconnected, dominated hand that plays very poorly versus an early/mid-position range (often crushed when we make a pair, limited equity when called, and hard to realize postflop). With plenty of stack depth, there’s no need to defend this wide; folding preserves chips for better spots and avoids negative-EV reverse implied odds situations.

KQs is a premium suited broadway hand that performs exceptionally well in position. Minni has shown significant aggression in previous hands, but also folded to a 3-bet in Hand #11. By raising to 900, I am exerting pressure to take the pot down pre-flop or to play a heads-up pot with the initiative and the best possible position. This also discourages the blinds from entering the pot cheaply or attempting a squeeze play.

76o is a weak hand, and I'm facing a 3-bet to 900 after Minni's initial raise. I'm out of position in the SB with a marginal hand that plays poorly against strong ranges. Calling 850 more with 76 offsuit against a 3-bet is burning chips. Easy fold.

Facing a 3-bet to 900 from Flash on the button after Minni's open-raise to 300 from middle position. My hand is 2d Th, which is a very weak offsuit hand with poor playability, low equity against likely strong 3-bet ranges (e.g., pairs, broadway, suited aces). Pot odds are about 1350:800 or roughly 1.7:1 direct, but potential multiway pot with Minni still to act behind, and I'm out of position. No reason to speculate with trash; folding preserves stack for better spots. Early in tournament (hand 18/150), no need to defend BB wide here.

I opened to 300 from MP with A♣8♦ to take the initiative. Flash (button) 3-bet to 900 — a standard-sized 3-bet, not an overkill. A8o in position is worth continuing: I have an ace-blocker, decent high-card value and good postflop playability on many flops. With ~106bb effective I prefer to keep the pot manageable and use position to c-bet A-high or dry boards for value and fold/control on wet/connected textures. 4‑betting to 1500 is too thin with this hand and would bloat the pot unnecessarily. I’ll call the 600 more (900 total committed) to see a flop in position and reassess if Flash applies heavy pressure or 4‑bets/shoves.

Flash 3‑bet preflop and then checked this dry flop (7♣ J♦ 5♠). I’m in position with A♣8♦ — I don’t have a pair, but I have a valuable Ace‑blocker and decent high‑card showdown value. Versus a 3‑bettor many hands missed this flop, so per my preflop plan I should take the initiative and c‑bet a small size to fold out equity and extract thin value from worse Ax and some broadways. I’ll keep the sizing modest with a ~1/3 pot continuation bet to avoid bloating the pot with a marginal hand; fold to a sizable check‑raise, and if called reassess on the turn (look to continue on safe runouts or pot‑control if checked to).

Minni has led into me (the pre-flop 3-better) with a small 'donk' bet of 650 into a pot of 1950. This often indicates a marginal hand like a mid-pair (88-TT) or a weak Jack seeking information or trying to set a cheap price to see the turn. I have two overcards (King and Queen) and a backdoor flush draw. Given the pot odds (calling 650 to win 2600), I have the required equity to continue, especially since I have position. Raising right now might be overplaying my hand against a range that could include sets or AJ, while calling allows me to realize my equity and potentially outplay her on later streets if she checks or if I hit one of my overcards.

I have A♣8♦ on 7c Jd 5s 3s — just ace-high. I c‑bet the flop (650) and the 3‑bettor called, so his range is weighted toward Jx, medium pairs and strong broadways that mostly beat my A‑high. The 3♠ is a blank for me and betting now would bloat the pot with very little chance to get called by worse hands (and would often get called or raised by hands that already beat me). With ~9.4k stack vs a 3.25k pot I prefer pot control and to realize equity in position — check and see a river. If he fires the river I can fold to sizable bets and re‑evaluate small bets, or take a free card if he checks back.

I currently hold King-high with two overcards. The board is relatively dry (7c-Jd-5s-3s). Minni led into me on the flop for a small amount (650 into 1950) and has now checked on the turn. This check often indicates either a marginal hand (like a mid-pair or a weak Jack) that is looking to get to showdown cheaply or a total air hand giving up. While a bet could potentially fold out middle pairs like 88-TT, Minni has shown a degree of stickiness and aggression in previous hands. By checking behind, I take a free card to try and hit a King or a Queen on the river, which would likely give me the best hand. It also prevents me from being check-raised off my equity and keeps the pot manageable.

Board is 7c Jd 5s 3s 6h; I have A♣8♦ — just ace‑high. I c‑bet the flop and got called by a pre‑flop 3‑better, who then checked the turn. That line (3‑bet preflop → call flop → check turn) is weighted to made hands (Jx, 7x, 55, maybe some overpairs or strong broadways) that beat A‑high; there are relatively few bluffs in his range to target. The river 6h is a blank for my hand and doesn’t improve a lot of likely calling hands. Betting here would mostly fold out the very weakest parts of his range but get called by most hands that beat me, so it’s a poor risk/reward play. I’ll check and realize my showdown equity rather than bluff into a caller-heavy range. Plan if he bets: call small, thin bets (~≤1/3 pot ≈ 1k) for pot odds against potential worse Ax or marginal pairs, but fold to larger river barrels (>~1/2 pot) where I’m almost never ahead.

The board is 7c-Jd-5s-3s-6h. I hold Kc-Qc for King-high. Pre-flop, I 3-bet and Minni called. On the flop, Minni led for 650 (about 1/3 pot) and I called with my overcards. The turn and river have gone check-check. Minni's line (donk-bet flop, check turn, check river) is highly indicative of a marginal hand like a mid-pair (88-TT) or a weak Jack that is afraid of my 3-betting range. While I could potentially bluff her off these hands with a significant bet, Minni has shown herself to be an active and sometimes 'sticky' player in previous hands. Since I have some showdown value with King-high against missed draws (like T9 or 89) and I want to preserve my stack early in the tournament, checking back is the most prudent play. I've already realized my equity and the pot is significant enough that a failed bluff would be costly.

UTG in 6-max with KcTd — a playable but somewhat marginal hand. I want to avoid multiway limp-pot disadvantage and seize fold equity, so I’ll open-raise. The table frequently opens to ~300 and stacks are deep (126BB), so I size to 3BB (300) rather than min-raising to 200 to discourage callers and define ranges. Postflop plan: c-bet on high-card/dry boards, proceed cautiously on coordinated or dominated boards; fold to large aggression when clearly 3‑bet/raised (KTo is often dominated). If I face only a small 3‑bet from a very loose player I may consider calling, otherwise fold to big 3‑bets.

UTG opened to 3bb into a 6-handed table, which represents a relatively strong range. Q4o is dominated by essentially all of Minni’s value and most of their continuing range, plays poorly postflop, and has no real redeeming blockers for a profitable 3-bet bluff here. With plenty of stack depth, best decision is to avoid marginal, dominated spots and preserve chips for better steal/3-bet opportunities from later position.

I am in the Cutoff position with 9s 2s. Minni has raised to 300 from UTG. 92s is a very weak hand that does not play well against a standard UTG opening range. Calling or raising with this hand would be highly unprofitable in the long run, as it is easily dominated and lacks significant post-flop potential. The correct and most disciplined play here is to fold.

J6o is a weak hand facing an UTG raise from Minni. Even with position on the button, this hand doesn't play well enough against an early position raise. Minni has been active and 3-bet me before (hand 13), so they're capable of playing back. Folding is the disciplined play here.

Hand 19, early in tournament with deep stacks (7750). In SB facing UTG raise to 300 (pot 450, to call 250). QsJh offsuit is mediocre: decent high cards but offsuit and poor playability OOP. UTG (Minni) range is strong (top 12-15%: pairs 88+, AJo+, KQo+, suited broadways). My equity ~35-40% vs that range, but OOP vs raiser and potential BB involvement makes it -EV. Folding preserves stack for better spots; SB defense too wide here risks multiway pots without initiative.

I am in the Big Blind with T6o facing a 3x raise from Minni in UTG. The pot odds are 200 to call into a pot of 450 (roughly 31% equity required). Even against a loose opening range, T6o has poor equity and very poor playability out of position. Given the aggressive nature of the opponent and my position, defending here is likely -EV due to reverse implied odds and difficulty realizing equity. Folding is the standard and prudent play.

UTG in 6-max with Q7o is too weak to open for value and performs poorly when called/3-bet. Limping invites isolation from aggressive players (Flash/Claude/Minni) and puts us in tough postflop spots out of position. With ~85bb effective, preserving stack and waiting for better open candidates is highest EV.

I am in middle position with Ace-Six of clubs. This is a strong starting hand in a 6-max game, especially suited. I have a healthy stack of 105 big blinds. Opening for a standard raise of 3x the big blind is consistent with my previous aggressive play and allows me to take control of the pot early or win the blinds and antes. Given the table dynamics, I want to maintain an active image.

I have ATs in the cutoff facing a raise from Flash in middle position. This is a solid hand that plays well in position. I'll 3-bet to around 850 to put pressure on Flash and isolate. ATs is strong enough to 3-bet for value/semi-bluff, and I've shown aggression in similar spots successfully.

Preflop on the button with 4s8d, a complete trash hand with no playability. Facing an open raise from Flash (MP) to 300 and a 3-bet from Claude (CO) to 850 into a 1300 pot. Even with position, 84o has negligible equity against strong 3-bet ranges (tight Ax, pairs, broadway). Calling 850 commits ~11% of my 7750 stack into a spot where I'm crushed postflop. Raising is suicidal with this hand. Folding preserves my stack for better opportunities in this early stage (hand 20/150). Opponents like Claude have been aggressive raisers historically, but doesn't change the fold.

I am in the Small Blind holding Jd 3h, which is a very weak hand. The action pre-flop has been heavy: Flash raised to 300 and Claude 3-bet to 850. Facing a 3-bet cold with junk cards while out of position is a standard fold situation. I have 9100 chips and there is no strategic reason to get involved in a bloated pot with this hand against two active opponents.

I’m in the big blind with 5s4h. Preflop Flash opened to 300 (MP) and Claude 3‑bet to 850 (CO). Pot is 1300 and I must call 750 (min 4‑bet to 1400). 5-4 offsuit is a weak hand with poor equity and playability against a CO 3‑bet (and likely the original raiser if they continue). Calling 750 into a 1300 pot gives a post-call pot of 2050, so I need roughly 36.6% equity to break even — far more than 5-4o typically has versus these ranges. A light 4‑bet bluff is thin (I lack useful blockers) and would put a lot of chips at risk in an early stage. Prefer to conserve stack and avoid a marginal, dominated spot. Fold.

I opened with a standard raise of 300 from middle position with Ace-Six of clubs. Claude, who has shown frequent aggression and 3-betting tendencies in previous hands, has 3-bet me to 850 from the cutoff. Playing A6s out of position in a 3-bet pot is a difficult spot; the hand is often dominated by better Aces or pairs, and I will be at a positional disadvantage for the rest of the hand. Given Claude's activity, I could consider a 4-bet bluff, but at this stage of the tournament, it's more prudent to fold and wait for a more favorable situation rather than committing more chips with a marginal hand.

I am UTG with Ace-Six offsuit. In a 6-handed game, A6o is generally considered too weak to open from the earliest position. It lacks the post-flop playability of suited connectors or stronger Aces and often finds itself dominated or out of position. Given the aggressive play observed from Claude and Minni in previous hands, I will stick to a tighter opening range and fold this marginal hand.

A3o in middle position after UTG fold. This is a marginal hand but with the ace, I can open raise to try to steal blinds. Standard 2.5x raise to 250 is appropriate here - not a premium hand but playable from this position.

Preflop in cutoff facing a 2.5x raise to 250 from Claude in middle position. My hand is QhTc offsuit, which is a marginal hand at best. It's queen-high with a weak offsuit ten kicker, vulnerable postflop to overcards and draws. While position is favorable, the hand doesn't play well multiway or against a strong MP opening range (which likely includes pairs, broadways, suited aces/connectors). Button (Pro) acts behind, plus blinds, so pot could get multiway. No strong history suggesting Claude is overly wide. Deep stacks (77BB), early in tournament (hand 21/150), no need to speculate with a weak hand that has poor playability. Standard fold to conserve stack for better spots.

I hold Kc 9s on the Button. Action is an open raise to 2.5BB from Claude in Middle Position. Claude has been active and aggressive, but an MP opening range typically includes hands that dominate K9o (AK, KQ, KJ, KT, 99+). While playing in position is advantageous, K9o has significant reverse implied odds and is unlikely to perform well against a standard opening range. Calling invites squeezes from the blinds or leads to tricky post-flop spots with a dominated top pair. Folding is the most prudent and +EV decision.

I’m in the SB with K♦5♥ facing a middle‑position open to 250 (pot 400, 200 to call). K5o is a weak, often‑dominated hand and I’ll be out of position postflop. Calling gives decent pot odds but creates difficult, low‑EV decisions (and may become multiway if the BB calls). 3‑betting as a bluff with offsuit K5 is thin against a MP opener. With ~126 BB effective I’ll conserve chips for better spots and fold preflop.

Claude opened from middle position and it’s folded to us in the big blind. With Jh5c (offsuit, disconnected), we have poor playability OOP versus an active opener and we’ll realize equity badly across multiple streets. While the pot odds are tempting, this hand sits below a profitable BB defend threshold versus an MP range, and turning it into a 3-bet bluff is also unattractive (weak blockers, dominated when called). Best is to conserve chips and fold.
K4o is a weak hand, especially from UTG position which requires a tight range. This hand has poor playability and will often be dominated by better kings. Folding is the correct play here.

Preflop in middle position with 9h6h suited connectors. UTG folded, unopened pot so far, pot 150, to call 100 (BB). Deep stacks (77BB effective). 96s is a speculative hand that plays best multiway with position and implied odds, but opening or calling here puts me out of position postflop against likely aggressive opponents like Flash, Claude, and Minni who frequently raise/3bet. Standard play is to fold marginal suited connectors from MP in unopened pots to avoid OOP awkward spots and preserve stack for premium opportunities. Tournament stage early (hand 22/150), no need to force action with this hand.

I hold Jd 7s in the Cutoff. This is a weak off-suit hand with poor playability and low equity. Although I am in late position, there are active players behind me, specifically Minni on the button and Flash in the Big Blind, who have shown aggression in previous hands. Opening light here risks getting 3-bet or called by better hands. The standard play with J7o is to fold and wait for a better spot.

I’m on the button with A♣7♣ — a playable, suited ace with good post‑flop playability and blocking value. With only the SB and BB to act, an open raise is standard: calling 100 lets both blinds see cheap flops and surrenders initiative. I’ll raise to 300 (3x the big blind). Size is big enough to apply pressure and isolate, but conservative relative to deep stacks (~126 BB) so I can play postflop. Plan: if called I’ll continuation‑bet favorable textures (A‑high, club draws, dry boards) and control pot size on dangerous boards; fold to a large, committed 3‑bet unless pot odds/reads justify continuing. The raise maximizes fold equity and extracts value from worse aces and club draws.

Facing a button open to 300 from the small blind, 4♠3♥ offsuit is too weak to defend profitably out of position: poor playability, no blockers, and we’ll realize equity badly versus a wide but still stronger opening range. A 3-bet bluff is unnecessary here without blockers and with a competent opponent who can continue often. Best decision is to preserve stack and fold.

I am in the Big Blind with 7h 5d. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While I am getting a decent price to call (200 more into a pot of 450), 7-5 offsuit is a very weak hand that plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position against an aggressive opponent like Minni. I'll choose to fold and wait for a better spot.

UTG with 9c3s offsuit is one of the worst starting hands possible. No high card value, no suitedness, no straight potential. In a 6-handed game early in the tournament with deep stacks, folding is mandatory to avoid playing out of position with trash. Preserving stack for better spots is key to long-term maximization.

I hold King-Queen offsuit (KQo) in Middle Position with 6 players remaining. After the UTG player folded, this is a strong hand well within the standard opening range for 6-max No-Limit Hold'em. My stack is healthy at 91bb. I will open-raise to 2.5bb (250 chips) to build the pot, thin the field, and capitalize on the strength of my hand. This sizing is consistent with my previous successful opens (e.g., Hand #17). Folding or limping here would be too passive given the hand strength.

Pocket fives in the cutoff facing a standard MP open to 2.5bb — with ~127 BB effective I want to set-mine rather than build a big pot out of position or risk getting 4-bet. Calling 250 preserves excellent implied odds to hit a set, keeps the pot manageable, and avoids a tiny reraise (to 400) that won’t fold out many hands or a large 3-bet that’s risky with a small pair. If players behind squeeze or there’s heavy 3-bet action I can fold; if we see a flop heads-up or single caller I’ll proceed to extract value when I hit or play cautiously as a pair of fives.

K2o is too weak and heavily dominated versus Pro’s MP opening range, and it plays poorly multiway with Minni already calling (reverse implied odds, tough to realize equity). A squeeze bluff here would need better blockers/playability, and calling invites difficult postflop spots. Preserve stack and wait for better BTN opportunities.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Four offsuit. Pro has raised from middle position and Minni has called. T4o is a very weak hand that plays poorly out of position, especially in a multi-way pot. Despite the decent pot odds to call (200 to win 650), the hand has almost no post-flop equity realization. The best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

K4o in BB facing a raise to 250 and a call from Minni. I need 150 to call into a pot of 650, getting about 4.3:1 odds. K4o is marginal but the pot odds are decent. I'll complete and see a flop, looking to hit top pair or fold to aggression.

I have K4o and completely missed this 3-7-6 flop. I have just king high with no pair, no draws. The board is low and coordinated with possible straight draws. I'm first to act with two opponents behind. Standard check here - no reason to bluff into two players with air. I'll check and likely fold to any aggression.

I opened pre-flop with KQo and got two callers (Minni in the CO and Claude in the BB). The flop comes 3c 7d 6s, which is a low, connected texture that connects far better with the calling ranges of the Big Blind and the Cutoff (who often have low pairs, suited connectors, or sets here) than my opening range of high cards. Being in a multi-way pot on a board disadvantageous to my range, my fold equity is reduced. Betting here is unlikely to get better hands to fold (like a pair of 6s or 7s) and opens me up to being raised or floated by hands with good equity. Checking allows me to control the pot size, realize my equity (seeing a free turn to potentially hit a King or Queen), and avoid putting money in bad spots. I have reasonable showdown value with King-high but not enough to value bet or comfortably semi-bluff into two opponents.

Check. I called preflop to set-mine and this flop (3c-7d-6s) largely misses my range — my pocket 5s are an underpair and vulnerable to any 7/6 or overcards. Both opponents checked, so I have very little fold equity and no reason to build a bigger pot out of position with a hand that wants to hit. Pot control is correct: see a cheap turn, fold to significant aggression, call small/medium bets to continue if the price is right, and only start extracting value if I improve (set or the rare 4 giving a straight).

I have K4o on 3-7-6-3 board - complete air with just king high. Everyone checked the flop showing weakness. The turn paired the 3s which doesn't change much. I could potentially bluff here since it looks like nobody connected, but betting with zero equity into two opponents is risky. Better to check and take a free card - maybe hit a king on the river. If someone bets, I'll fold.

On the turn, the board pairs with the Three of Hearts, making it [3c 7d 6s 3h]. The action checked to me again after both the Big Blind and I checked the flop, and the Cutoff checked back. The pot remains small (800). My King-Queen high still has decent equity (6 outs to top pair) but likely no showdown value currently against two opponents on a low board that favors their defending ranges (pairs of 6s, 7s, or pocket pairs). Since both opponents showed weakness on the flop, they might be trapping or simply have marginal made hands like second or third pair that they will not fold to a single bet. Betting here turns my hand into a bluff with low success probability multi-way. Checking back allows me to realize my equity for free, seeing a river card that could give me top pair, or potentially winning at showdown if everyone missed. I will maintain pot control.

I called preflop to set-mine with pocket 5s and have not improved. The turn paired the board (3c-7d-6s-3h), which increases the chance someone holds trips or two pair (7x/6x) and makes any bluff thin — both opponents checked the turn showing either weakness or pot control. With an underpair in a three-way pot I have virtually no fold equity and should keep the pot small. I check to control the pot and see the river. If I hit a set on the river I will extract value; if I don’t improve I will fold to significant aggression and only consider calling very small bets for pot odds.

River brought an Ace on a 3-7-6-3-A board. I have K4o - just king high. Everyone checked flop and turn showing weakness. The Ace could have helped someone who was slow-playing Ax. Betting as a bluff into two players is risky when the Ace might have connected. I'll check and see if my king-high takes it down - if someone bets, I'll fold.

The river brings an Ace, which connects well with my pre-flop opening range (AK, AQ, AJ) and serves as a potential scare card. However, the pot is multi-way with two opponents. Although both opponents have shown weakness by checking previous streets, their ranges likely consist of marginal made hands like mid-pairs (6x, 7x, 88-TT) that might hero-call a river bet, or they could have trapped. Betting turns my hand into a bluff that needs to get through two players. While I have little showdown value with King-high, checking is the lower-variance play to preserve my stack rather than firing a bluff into a board where opponents likely connected with a pair.

I never improved my pocket fives and the river A only makes my hand worse — hands like A‑x, 7x, 6x or trips beat me. This is a three‑way pot and both opponents showed pot control by checking the turn, so my fold equity is essentially zero; any bet I make will be called by better marginal hands or the rare bluff catcher, and I risk bloating the pot out of position. Priority is pot control and stack preservation: check and take the showdown. If anyone fires a small probe I can consider a call for pot odds, but I will fold to significant aggression.

I am in UTG position with 4h 2h, which is a very weak suited connector (one-gapper). Being first to act with 5 players behind me requires a strong range to open profitably. This hand does not meet the criteria for an opening range from Early Position. My stack is healthy (88.5 BBs), so there is no need to make loose speculative plays. Folding is the standard and correct decision.

K♠3♥ is a weak offsuit king with a terrible kicker and poor postflop playability. I’m in middle position with several players yet to act — this hand will often be dominated and is not worth investing chips, even just calling 1 BB. A raise to attempt a steal is unjustified with this holding. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot, so fold.

Folded to me in the cutoff with K5o. While this is a tempting steal spot, K5o is a weak offsuit king that performs poorly when called/3-bet (dominated and reverse implied odds). The players behind—especially Button/SB—have shown aggression and willingness to apply pressure preflop, which reduces the profitability of a light open. I’ll pass and preserve stack for better steal candidates.

I am on the button with 7h 4s, which is a very weak hand. While it's folded to me and I could potentially attempt to steal the blinds, this specific hand has very poor playability if called, and the players in the blinds have shown willingness to play. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack.
Everyone folded to me in the SB with KTs. This is a strong hand heads up against just the BB. I should raise to put pressure on Elon and try to take down the pot or build value.

Preflop in the big blind facing a small blind raise to 300 after all others folded. Pot is 400, to call 200 more (total 300 committed). Holding 5s6s, suited connectors with good playability, straight and flush potential. Claude has been aggressive preflop from history (frequent raises and 3bets), suggesting a wide opening range from SB (~40-50% hands). 56s has solid equity (~40%+) against such a range and excellent implied odds given deep stacks (mine 76BB, Claude 113BB). Pot odds are favorable (200 into 600 post-call = 33% required equity). Folding is too tight in BB defense spot; 3-betting risks playing OOP with marginal hand postflop. Calling keeps pot small, realizes equity well postflop where suited connectors shine, especially vs Cbet-heavy opponent. Plan to play aggressively on favorable boards (straights, flushes, pairs with draws).

I raised preflop with KTs and got called by the BB. Flop is As 5d Js - I missed but have a gutshot to Broadway (need Q) and a backdoor spade flush draw with my Ts. As the preflop aggressor, this ace-high board favors my range. A standard c-bet around 1/3 pot should take this down often enough to be profitable.

Flop board As5dJs is very favorable: I flopped middle pair (5s), a strong spade flush draw (three spades total with two on board), and gutshot straight draw potential with 6 (e.g., to 7 for wheel-ish but more blockers). Equity is massive (~60%+ vs Claude's SB cbet range, which includes Ax, Jx, bluffs, weak pairs, draws). He cbets small 200 into 800 (~25% pot), typical vs BB defense range, often weak/ polarized. Pot odds excellent (200 into 1000 ~20% equity needed). Calling realizes equity well OOP but allows free cards. Raising extracts value from his worse pairs/draws/Ax without top pair, denies equity to his draws (e.g., spade draws without pair, KTs), builds pot with my strong hand (~70% equity realized), and takes initiative. Minraise too small; sizing to ~3x his bet (600 total) balances value/protection without overbetting deep. Stacks deep (74BB), so room for postflop flexibility. Claude aggressive but folds weak cbets often per history. Plan: If he folds often, great; calls, barrel turns with equity/stronger made hands.

Elon check-raised my c-bet from 200 to 600. This shows strength - likely an ace, a jack, or a strong draw. I have Kh Ts with only a gutshot straight draw (need Q) and the Ts gives me a weak backdoor flush draw. Pot is 1400 and I need 400 to call, getting ~3.5:1. My gutshot has only ~8% equity which isn't enough even with implied odds against a player showing this much strength. Folding is disciplined here - no need to chase with limited equity.

6-max UTG with QJo (Qh Jd) — playable, has decent equity and postflop playability in a short-handed game. Limping is weak from this seat; a standard open is appropriate. I’ll open to a 3x BB sizing (300) to take the initiative, get fold equity, and build the pot when I’m likely ahead versus blinds. Versus a 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate (can call small 3-bets with position plans, fold to large 4-bets/shoves), but preflop I want to be the aggressor here.

Minni’s UTG 3x open in a 6-handed game represents a relatively strong, tight range. T9 offsuit plays poorly versus that range (dominated by better broadways, weak top-pair potential, and significant reverse implied odds), and calling also invites squeezes from the button/cutoff/blinds. With ~80BB effective, this is a clear discipline fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am in the Cutoff with Ace-Deuce offsuit. Minni opened from UTG for 300. While Minni has been playing many hands, Ace-Deuce is quite weak and easily dominated by a standard opening range. Additionally, there are three players left to act behind me, including the Button, which increases the risk of being squeezed or playing out of position. Folding is the most prudent play here.

76o is a marginal speculative hand facing a UTG raise from Minni who has been aggressive. While I have position on the button, this hand doesn't play well enough against a UTG opening range. Minni has shown willingness to 4-bet (hand #13), making a light 3-bet risky. Better to fold and wait for a stronger spot.

Preflop from the small blind facing an UTG raise to 300 from Minni, who has shown aggression in prior hands including 3-bets and 4-bets. My hand is 5s4h, a weak offsuit low connector with poor playability out of position. Pot is 450, to call 250 offers about 1.8:1 but doesn't justify entering with this hand OOP against a strong range. No fold equity squeeze opportunity with 6 players left. Best to fold and wait for better spots to maximize stack long-term.

I am in the Big Blind with Q5o facing a 3bb raise from Minni in UTG. The pot odds are roughly 30% (calling 200 to win a total pot of 650), but Q5o performs very poorly against a standard strong UTG opening range. Being out of position with a dominated hand creates significant reverse implied odds. The standard play is to fold.

6-handed UTG ranges are wider, and Q8s has reasonable playability (suited, can make strong top pairs/flushes) and benefits from taking initiative. Limping is dominated and invites squeezes; folding is a bit tight given stack depth (~84bb) and table dynamics. I’ll open with a standard size that pressures the blinds but keeps my range consistent, planning to fold versus strong 3-bets and continue versus calls on favorable textures.

I have a small pocket pair (4c, 4h) in middle position. FiveTwo opened for 250 from UTG. With effective stacks over 80 big blinds, this is a standard spot to call for set-mining value. The goal is to flop a set of fours; if I miss and face aggression, I can easily fold, but the potential payout if I hit justifies the 2.5bb call.
8h3h is a weak suited hand. Facing a UTG raise and a caller, with players still behind me, this is an easy fold. The hand lacks playability and has poor implied odds even when suited.

27o is a complete trash hand with no playability, straight draw potential, or suitedness. Facing an UTG raise from FiveTwo (who has shown aggression but typically strength early position) and a middle position flat call from Flash, the range is strong. Even on the button with deep stacks, calling 250 into 650 pot commits to a multiway pot OOP postflop with a hand that hits flops rarely and poorly. SB and BB still to act behind, increasing risk. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this long tournament.

I am in the Small Blind with KQs, a premium suited broadway hand. Facing a UTG raise to 250 (2.5BB) from FiveTwo and a flat call from Flash in Middle Position, I have a decision between calling and 3-betting (squeezing). While FiveTwo has been active and has folded to 3-bets previously (Hand #5), an open from UTG represents a stronger range than from late position. Squeezing from the SB is a high-variance play that risks being 4-bet by a premium hand (AA, KK, AK) in the UTG range, forcing me to fold significant equity. Calling allows me to close the action relative to the raiser (subject to BB), giving me excellent direct pot odds (calling 200 to win a pot of 650+) and implied odds. KQs plays well in multi-way pots with flush and straight capabilities. Given my deep stack (87 BB) and position (OOP), keeping the pot manageable and retaining dominated hands in Flash's calling range is the prudent, lower-variance line compared to bloating the pot out of position.

Fold. UTG opened to 250 and there are two callers already (SB and Flash) — this is a multiway pot and I’ll be out of position postflop. I have K♥6♣: a marginal, often-dominated Kx hand with a weak kicker. Calling 150 to play multiway gives poor playability and little implied edge; 3-betting as a bluff is weak here since I lack fold equity and won’t play well against an UTG opener. With a healthy 13,375 stack (~134 BB) I should conserve chips and wait for a better, cleaner spot.

I hold KsQs on a Jd 2h Ac board. I have a gutshot straight draw to the broadway straight (need a Ten) and a backdoor spade flush draw. I am out of position in a 3-way pot against the UTG raiser and a caller. This board texture heavily favors the UTG opener's range (which contains all strong Aces and sets like JJ/AA). Leading out (donk betting) would likely only get called by better hands and fold out worse ones. The standard and most prudent play is to check to the pre-flop aggressor to see how they proceed. I will evaluate whether to continue based on the sizing if a bet is made.

This is a 3-way flop on A♣ J♦ 2♥. While the ace-high texture is good for my UTG opening range in theory, bluffing multiway requires much more equity and fold equity than heads-up. With Q♣8♠ I have no made hand, only a thin overcard to the J plus a weak backdoor club possibility, and I don’t want to bloat the pot and get check-raised or called in two spots where I’ll be forced to shut down on many turns. Checking keeps the pot manageable, protects my checking range (I can check some strong Ax too), and lets me realize equity / potentially continue if a favorable turn (Q, club, etc.) appears or if the action stays passive.

The flop is Ace-high, which hits a lot of the pre-flop raiser's (FiveTwo) range. However, both the small blind and FiveTwo checked. My pocket fours have very little showdown value and are currently an underpair. I will check behind to see a free turn card, hoping to hit a set or see if the hand stays passive so I can evaluate on the next street.

On the Turn, the board is [Jd 2h Ac Kc]. I have improved to second pair (Kings) with a strong kicker (Queen) and retain my gutshot straight draw (needing a Ten). The pot is 850. The flop checked through, which suggests weakness or pot control from the UTG raiser (FiveTwo) and the MP caller (Flash). However, the King on the turn hits the UTG range hard (AK, KK, KQ, QT). While I have showdown value, leading out (donk betting) into two opponents on this wet board (possible straights with QT or any Ten) is risky; betting likely folds out worse hands and gets called by better (Ax, sets, straights). Checking allows me to control the pot size, keeping my range wide and reacting to the opponents' actions. If it checks through, I have a decent hand for showdown or improving on the river.

Turn brings Kc giving us a very strong combo draw: the nut club flush draw (Qc is the highest possible hole club with Ac/Kc on board) plus a gutshot to Broadway (T). After the flop checks through multiway, both callers are capped toward a lot of medium-strength hands (Jx, weak Ax, pocket pairs) and some traps, but overall they’ll have trouble continuing versus pressure on this A-K texture that heavily favors my UTG opening range. Betting also denies a free river and gives us two clean ways to win: folds now or hitting a big hand on the river. I’ll use a solid, fold-inducing size without overcommitting.

I have a pair of fours, which is currently an underpair on a board of Jack-Deuce-Ace-King. This board is extremely favorable for the pre-flop raiser's (FiveTwo) range, and my hand has almost no chance of being ahead or improving to the best hand. FiveTwo checked the flop but is now leading into two opponents on the turn. My pocket fours have no showdown value here, and there is still another player (Pro) to act behind me. Folding is the only sensible play.

I hold KQs and am facing a delayed c-bet from the UTG raiser on a wet Turn board of [Jd 2h Ac Kc]. Although I have improved to second pair with a strong kicker, the board texture heavily favors the UTG range, which includes all sets (AA, KK, JJ), strong two-pair combos (AK, AJ), and the straight (QT). The opponent's check-back on the Ace-high flop followed by a bet on the King turn indicates significant strength—likely a slow-played monster or a hand that just improved (like AK or KK). I am losing to almost all value hands in their range and only beating pure bluffs, which are rare on this coordinated board in a multi-way pot. Calling out of position with second pair is a low-equity spot. Preserving my stack is the correct play.

I am in the Under the Gun (UTG) position with Ace-Seven of spades. In a 6-max tournament format, A-Xs is a standard opening hand to exert pressure and build a pot with a hand that has good post-flop potential, specifically for nut-flush draws and top-pair strength. I will raise to 300, which is a standard 3x big blind open.

J8o is a weak hand facing an UTG raise of 300. UTG raises typically represent strong ranges, and J8 offsuit doesn't have good playability - it's easily dominated by better jacks and doesn't have suited potential for flush draws. With 4 players yet to act behind me, there's squeeze potential. This is a clear fold.

Preflop, UTG open to 300 (3x BB) from Flash, who has been aggressive in prior hands but with deep stacks (95BB effective). QdTd is a strong suited connector from Cutoff, with excellent playability postflop: flush potential, straight draws, and position on button, blinds. Calling maintains pot control, realizes equity multiway if called behind, and avoids bloating pot OOP vs UTG strength. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks domination or isolation OOP for opponent. Pot odds favorable (450 pot, 300 call). Plan to play aggressively postflop with initiative.

I hold Jack-Deuce offsuit on the button, which is a statistically very weak hand. The action began with an UTG raise from Flash, indicating strength, followed by a call from Elon in the Cutoff. Despite having position, my hand has very poor equity and playability against these ranges. There is no justification for calling or 3-betting with this holding. The standard and correct play is to fold immediately.

Facing an UTG raise to 300 and a call from Elon, I’m in the small blind with 3♣10♣ (3c Tc). The pot is 750 and I must add 250 to call (min raise to 500). This is a multiway, out-of-position situation against an UTG open (likely a stronger, tighter range) plus a caller — T3s is often dominated and has poor postflop playability here. Calling 250 requires roughly ~25% equity versus the current pot to be profitable, and a min-raise would be a light and risky isolation attempt. With a deep stack I can wait for a better, in-position spot or stronger hand. Fold and preserve chips.

Facing an UTG open to 3bb and a cold-call, 8♣3♥ is too weak to defend profitably: poor playability, dominated pair potential, and we’ll be out of position multiway with little ability to realize equity. A squeeze bluff into an UTG range plus a caller is also low-EV at these stacks. Best is to preserve chips and fold.

I have top pair, top kicker (As7s) on a 7h-5h-3d flop. This is a very strong hand in a heads-up pot against the cutoff. I will lead out with a continuation bet to get value from draws (both flush and straight) and middle pairs, while also protecting my hand against potential overcards on the turn. A bet of 400 into the 750 pot is a standard size that accomplishes these goals.

Flop of 7h5h3d completely misses QdTd. No pair, no straight or flush draw (only backdoor diamond flush draw with three diamonds total and two overcards). Flash's UTG raise + c-bet into 1150 pot for 400 (pot odds ~2.9:1, need 26% equity) on this drawy board (two hearts possible flush draw). Our equity vs. his strong range is ~15-25% at best, with little showdown value and poor playability going forward. Position helps but not enough to justify a float OOP-equivalent heads-up vs. aggressive Flash (history of UTG opens/cbets). Preserve deep stack (79BB) for better spots; fold.
6s7h from UTG is a weak offsuit connector. UTG is the tightest position and this hand doesn't have the playability needed to open from here - not suited, not high cards. Easy fold.

AKo is a premium hand in middle position after one fold. Standard play is to raise to isolate and build the pot while taking initiative. Raise sizing around 3x BB (300) is common based on previous hands and deep stacks. Folding or limping is suboptimal as it misses value and fold equity.

I am in the Cutoff with King-Jack offsuit (KJo). The player in Middle Position (Elon) has raised to 300 (3BB). KJo is a marginal hand that is easily dominated by an MP opening range (which typically includes AK, AQ, AJ, KQ, and pocket pairs). Playing this hand often leads to 'reverse implied odds' situations where I might make top pair but lose to a better kicker. Additionally, there are still players left to act behind me (Button, SB, BB) who could squeeze or wake up with a premium hand. Given the 3x open size indicating reasonable strength and my position relative to the raiser, folding is the prudent play to preserve chips for better spots.

I’m on the button with 8♥3♥ facing a 3x open to 300 from middle position. 8-3 suited is one of the weakest playable hands — poor equity, easy to be dominated, and has very limited postflop playability. There are still the small and big blinds to act, so this is likely to get multiway and my equity collapses further. A call is cheap relative to my stack, but I don’t want to invest in a marginal, often-dominated hand out of position vs the raiser’s range; a 3-bet bluff is also thin here (no meaningful blockers and likely to be called by better hands). Long-term I preserve chips and wait for a cleaner spot/stronger holding on the button, so I fold.

Elon opens to 3bb from middle position, and we’re in the small blind out of position with 9♣4♠ (a dominated, poorly connected offsuit hand). Calling invites the big blind in and puts us in tough postflop spots with weak equity and little playability. A 3-bet bluff is also too ambitious with this combo given how far down it is and our positional disadvantage. Best EV is to cut losses and preserve stack.

I am in the Big Blind with 9h 6d. Elon has raised to 300 from middle position. While I am getting a decent price to call (200 more to win a total pot of 450), 9-6 offsuit is a very weak hand that realizes equity poorly out of position. Defensive calls with hands like this usually lead to difficult post-flop situations, especially against a standard opening range. I have a healthy stack and see no reason to defend with such a marginal hand.

UTG in a 6-handed game with K7s, a marginal suited hand with poor playability and dominated potential. Standard tight range preflop from early position: fold speculative hands like this to avoid tough postflop spots OOP with a weak king. Deep stacks, no reads justifying a limp or raise. Preserve stack for better spots.

I hold Q5o in middle position (UTG+1 effectively). This is a very weak hand that falls well outside standard opening ranges, especially with four players left to act behind me. With 85 BBs, there is no pressure to steal, and playing this hand would likely lead to a dominated situation or a difficult post-flop spot out of position. The correct play is to fold.

Cutoff with Js6s — a suited Jack with decent playability and a spade blocker. With deep stacks (~133BB) and position on the button and blinds, I want to take the initiative and try to steal the blinds / play post‑flop in position. I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300) to reduce multiway pots and generate fold equity. Plan: if faced with a large cold 3‑bet I’ll fold; I’ll call smaller 3‑bets selectively depending on who 3‑bets and the size. Postflop I’ll continue aggressively on favorable cards (Jx, spade/straight draws) and control pot size on dry boards.

Minni opens from the cutoff to 300 and we’re on the button with 7-2 offsuit, which has extremely poor playability and realization even in position. Calling burns chips and invites multiway action from the blinds; 3-betting as a bluff is too low-quality with this hand (bad blockers, dominated when called, poor postflop). Best EV is to preserve stack and wait for better spots in position.

Minni opened from the cutoff with a raise to 300. Holding Queen-Six offsuit in the small blind, I am out of position against an active opponent with a larger stack. This hand does not meet the requirements for a profitable call or a 3-bet bluff, especially with an aggressive player like Claude in the big blind yet to act. Folding is the most disciplined and standard play here.

T3o is a weak hand with poor playability. Facing a 3x raise from Minni in the cutoff, I need to call 200 into a 450 pot. While the odds are decent, T3o doesn't have enough equity or postflop potential to defend profitably. Easy fold.

I am in UTG position with Qc 7c. In a 6-handed game, this hand is significantly below the standard opening range for Under the Gun. The hand has potential flush value but is easily dominated by better Queens or stronger suited connectors. With 5 opponents left to act, the risk of running into a stronger hand or facing a 3-bet is high. Preserving my 85BB stack is prioritized over playing a marginal suited gapper out of position.

I have Ac9d in middle position in a 6-max game with UTG folded. This is a standard open-raise hand — the Ace with a decent kicker has good equity and an Ace-blocker, and I should be taking the initiative rather than limping. With deep stacks (~135 BBs) and the table tendency to open around 2.5–3x, I’ll raise to 3bb (300) to build the pot and apply pressure to the cutoff/button and the blinds. If I face a large 3-bet from the blinds I’ll likely fold (A9o is not a 4-bet hand), but if I get single calls I’m comfortable playing position and continuing on many flops.

Minni opens to 3x from middle position and we’re in the CO with 96 offsuit. This hand is too weak to profitably continue versus a relatively strong opening range: it’s frequently dominated (by 9x and better), has poor playability in single-raised pots at 3x sizing, and makes weak pairs/straights that are hard to realize equity with against multiple players behind who can squeeze. A 3-bet bluff is also unattractive with no premium blockers and mediocre postflop prospects if called. Preserve stack and wait for better spots in position.

I am on the button with a pocket pair (4d, 4s). Minni has opened from middle position for 300. My stack is deep (101bb), and the pot odds for calling are 2.5:1. Pocket pairs have significant implied value if I flop a set, especially against an aggressive opener like Minni. Calling allows me to see a flop in position with a hand that plays well if it hits, while keeping the pot manageable if it doesn't.

I have 3c4c in the SB - a weak suited connector. Minni raised and Flash called. I need 250 to call into a 750 pot (3:1). While suited connectors can flop well multiway, 34s is at the bottom of playable hands. I'm out of position against two opponents, and Elon could still squeeze behind me. The implied odds aren't great enough to justify playing this weak holding OOP. Folding is the disciplined play.

Pocket deuces is a standard defensive hand in the big blind facing a 3x open from middling position and a button call. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into a 750 pot (~3.75:1 direct, better implied). Deep stacks (mine 7950, others 9k+) provide strong set-mining implied odds. Multiway pots favor sets over overpairs or big cards. Raising risks isolation OOP or domination; folding too tight. Call and play postflop cautiously, looking to hit a set.

On the flop of 8h Ad Ts, I hold 22, a small underpair in a multiway pot (Minni's MP raise, Flash's BTN call, my BB call/set mine). No improvement, vulnerable to any bet from better pairs, Ax, Tx, or draws (straight draws like J9, flush draw hearts). OOP as last to act after presumed checks (pot unchanged at 950, to-call 0). Betting risks isolation with weak hand; raising pointless. Check to pot control, realize equity cheaply if checked through, or fold to bets. Deep stacks but no strong implied odds post-miss. Standard check.

I opened to 300 preflop and got two callers (Button and BB). Flop is 8♥ A♦ T♠ and I hold A♣9♦ — top pair with a medium kicker. The board is fairly dry (rainbow) — main worries are two‑pair (AT, A8, sets) or better A‑x kickers, but many of their preflop call ranges are wide and will check back weaker hands or floats. Both opponents checked to me, showing weakness or passive play. With position and a hand that wants protection/value, I should continuation‑bet to charge draws and get value from worse aces and medium pairs. I’ll size to about half the pot (~475) to extract value while not bloating the pot too much out of position vs potential stronger aces. If I face a large raise I’ll give it up (A9o is not a 3‑bet/commit hand); if I get one caller I’ll re‑evaluate on the turn.

I have pocket fours on a board of A-T-8. Minni, the pre-flop aggressor, has fired a continuation bet of half the pot. This board texture hits a standard opening range extremely hard, containing many superior pairs and top-pair combinations. I did not flop a set, and my hand has almost no potential to improve significantly on future streets. Calling here would be 'chasing' with very little equity, especially with Elon still left to act behind me. The most disciplined and profitable play in this situation is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Facing a 475 bet into 1425 pot from Minni, who raised pre-flop from middle position and now c-bets a dry-ish Ad Ts 8h board. Pocket 22 is a weak underpair with no showdown value, no draws, and poor equity (~12-18% vs her likely Ax, Tx, 99-JJ, sets, or draws like QJ/J9). Pot odds are ~4:1 but insufficient given reverse implied odds OOP heads-up deep; calling risks stacking off to better hands later or folding to turns/rivers. Folding realizes we set-mined pre-flop profitably (got a cheap flop) but missed, now best to exit cleanly. No reason to raise bluff or call.

QcJc is a strong, playable hand in 6-max from UTG — worth opening. Stacks are deep (≈141bb) so I want to take the initiative, get fold equity, and avoid letting too many players see a cheap flop. A standard 3x open is consistent with table tendencies and keeps pot size reasonable postflop. Plan: open-raise to 300. If called, c-bet on favorable flops and play for value/control; if I face a large 3-bet (especially from BTN/SB/BB > ~700–800), I will fold or proceed cautiously depending on sizing and opponent; against a small 3-bet from the blinds I may call to play postflop.

Minni’s UTG 3x open in 6-max represents a relatively strong range. T5o has poor raw equity, is dominated by most of the continuing range, and plays badly postflop (reverse implied odds, few strong draws). With plenty of stack depth, this is a clear spot to preserve chips and wait for better positions/holdings rather than defend too wide versus UTG.

I am holding 2s 4d, which is a extremely weak hand. Minni has raised to 300 from UTG, indicating a strong range or at least a standard opening. With three players left to act behind me and a hand with almost zero playability or showdown value, folding is the only logical move to preserve my stack.

76s on the button facing UTG raise from Minni (who has been active). I have position and suited connectors play well postflop with implied odds. Getting about 2.5:1 on the call with position. Worth seeing a flop.

A6o is a marginal hand at best, offsuit with a weak kicker. Facing an UTG raise from Minni, who has shown aggression but likely has a strong range here (top 15-20% or so), and a button call from Claude, which could be wide but still adds strength to the pot. From the SB, I'm OOP postflop with poor playability—no straight/flush potential, dominated by AT+, AQ+, pairs. Pot odds are ~3:1 (250 to call into 750), but implied odds are limited due to domination and position. BB still to act and could squeeze. Raising would be a bluff with bad blockers and poor equity. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots in a long tournament (31/150 hands).

Minni has been opening very frequently, but even against a wide UTG range, A3o is a difficult hand to play out of position in a multi-way pot. Claude's flat call on the button adds another opponent with position on me. While the pot odds are attractive (calling 200 to win 750, ~4.75:1), the reverse implied odds of hitting a dominated Ace and the low playability of the hand make it a losing proposition in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

I opened UTG and Claude (BTN) called. Flop Td 2c 4h is very dry and misses a big portion of his calling range. I hold QcJc — two overcards and a runner-runner club draw but no made hand. As the preflop aggressor I should continue often to seize the initiative and fold out unpaired hands. A small c-bet (~1/3 pot = 250) gives good fold equity, controls the pot OOP with deep stacks, and preserves maneuverability for the turn. Plan: take it down if he folds; if he calls I have reasonable equity with my overcards and will re-evaluate on the turn (raise from him = likely fold unless I improve; call and proceed cautiously if he just flats).
Missed the flop completely with 76 - no pair, no straight draw, no flush draw. Just two overcards to the board that don't help me. Even with a small c-bet, I have virtually no equity here. Easy fold.

UTG 6-handed, 8♠5♦ offsuit (actually 8h5d) is too weak to enter: dominated when we make a pair, poor playability multiway, and we’ll be out of position the rest of the hand. With plenty of stack depth, there’s no need to bleed chips limping or opening this hand from UTG.

Ten-Eight offsuit is too weak a hand to open from middle position in a 6-max game. With several players behind me, including an aggressive Big Blind (Minni), it is best to fold and wait for a stronger starting hand.
7h4d is a weak offsuit hand with poor connectivity. From the cutoff with 3 players behind, this doesn't meet the threshold for an open raise. Folding is the correct play.

Unopened pot on the button after UTG, MP, and CO folds. Q9o is a profitable open-raising hand from the button due to position advantage, potential to steal blinds (pot is 150, calling costs 100 for ~2:1 odds), and playability postflop. Stacks are deep (77BB effective), so standard 3x raise to 300 total (2x over the BB). Limping is suboptimal as it denies equity realization and invites multiway pots awkwardly. Folding too tight in this spot misses value.

I am in the Small Blind with Js 4h, a very weak off-suit hand. Elon raised from the Button to 300. Playing J4o out of position against a Button raise is a losing strategy due to poor equity and playability. The pot odds do not justify a call, and the hand is too weak for a 3-bet bluff. The standard and correct play is to fold.

9♥2♥ is a very weak holding despite being suited. Elon opened from the button to 300 (3x); I’m out of position and face a wide, but often strong, opening range that dominates my hand. Calling 200 more puts me in difficult multi-street decisions OOP with little equity and frequent domination. A light 3-bet bluff is unattractive here — poor blockers and a real chance of getting called or 4-bet by deeper stacks. With a healthy stack and plenty of hands left in the tournament, the +EV play is to preserve chips and fold.

I am holding Ace-Queen suited (Ah Qh), which is a premium starting hand in a 6-max game. Although I am in the Under the Gun (UTG) position, this hand is well within my opening range. I will raise to 300, which is consistent with my previous opening sizes in this tournament, to build a pot and take control of the hand pre-flop.
T5o is a weak hand - offsuit, not connected, facing a 3x raise from UTG. This is an easy fold from middle position.

Facing a standard UTG open to 3BB from Flash, who has shown aggression in prior hands. QTo offsuit in cutoff position: decent speculative hand with position, playability for straights, and blocker to Qx hands. Equity vs typical UTG range (~12-15%) is around 38%, and postflop position is advantageous for realizing equity. Folding too tight given deep stacks (79BB) and tournament goal to accumulate chips. 3-betting risks multiway pot OOP with marginal hand; prefer calling to control pot size and play IP. Expect button and blinds to defend wide potentially, but QTo plays decently multiway.

I hold 9d 7s on the Button. The action began with an UTG raise to 300 (3BB) from Flash, followed by a call from Elon in the Cutoff. My hand (97 offsuit) is very weak, disconnected, and unsuited. Although I have position, facing an UTG opening range (which is typically strong) and a caller makes this a very unprofitable spot to enter. I do not have the equity to call, nor do I have blockers or playability to justify a squeeze play. The standard play is to fold.

Fold. I’m in the small blind with Q♠6♦ facing an UTG open to 300 that’s already picked up a caller (Elon). Q6o is weak and often dominated by an UTG raising range and by the caller — multiway equity is poor and I’ll be OOP throughout. Calling 250 into ~750 only gives ~25% pot odds but my actual equity vs these ranges is lower, and with deep stacks there’s no reason to gamble marginal chips here. A 3-bet bluff is possible in some spots, but versus an early-position opener and a live caller it’s too risky; fold and wait for a better spot.

We’re getting a very good price closing the action: calling 200 to win a pot that will be 950 gives us ~21% immediate equity needed. 5s6s is a suited connector with strong playability and implied odds (straights/flushes) in a multiway pot, and we can realize equity reasonably well from the BB when we’re last preflop. A squeeze here is not attractive: we lack blockers, are up against an UTG opening range plus a caller, and would often get called and play a bloated pot out of position with a marginal hand. Folding would be too tight given the price and connectivity.

Flop is Kd-2s-7h, a dry high-card board that strongly favors UTG’s preflop raising range (lots of Kx/overpairs) and is not great for our 5s6s: no made hand, no immediate straight draw, only weak backdoor straight/flush potential. In a 3-way pot, leading (donk-betting) is unlikely to fold out better and can isolate us in a bloated pot out of position. Best is to check and respond to sizing: comfortably fold to meaningful pressure, continue only versus very small bets or on turns that improve equity (spade/4/3/8/9) after checks.

I was the pre-flop aggressor with Ace-Queen suited. The flop comes King-high and very dry (Kd-2s-7h). As the UTG raiser, my range is perceived to be strong and includes many Kings (AK, KQ). I will place a standard continuation bet of 450, roughly half the pot, to put pressure on my opponents and potentially take down the pot immediately. Even if called, I maintain overcard equity to the board's lower cards.

Flop Kd 2s 7h completely misses QTo offsuit: no pair, no draws (rainbow board, no flush draw, no straight possibilities with T-Q). Facing UTG Flash's standard continuation bet of 450 into ~950 pot (now 1400) after BB check. Flash's UTG range hits this K-high board frequently (Kx, 77, 22, some Ax), and even polarized includes bluffs but equity realization low multiway (BB still in). Position as cutoff is IP but calling 450 (~1/3 pot) requires ~25% equity minimum for implied odds, but Q-high has ~12-18% vs typical c-bet range. Poor pot odds, no fold equity on call, better to fold and preserve deep stack (79BB) for premium spots. Consistent with preflop plan to play postflop selectively.

Flop Kd-2s-7h is very favorable for UTG’s range (Kx, overpairs, strong broadways) and very poor for 5s6s: no pair, no direct straight draw, only weak backdoor straight possibilities plus a backdoor spade flush (need runner-runner). Versus 450 into 1400, we’d need ~24% equity to call, but our realistic equity and realization OOP are far lower, and we’ll face turn barrels frequently. A check-raise bluff is also unattractive here: we have no relevant blockers to Kx/overpairs and would be risking too much with limited ability to improve. Best play is to cut losses and fold.
ATs is a solid opening hand from UTG in 6-handed. Good equity and playability. Standard raise to 250.

K4o is a trash hand, offsuit, poor kicker, no playability. Facing an UTG raise from Claude (to 250 into 400 pot), which indicates a strong range. I'm in middle position, out of position postflop, with deep stacks. Calling would be speculative with poor equity multiway. Raising is a bluff with no blockers. Best to fold and wait for better spots to build stack.

I hold 8h 5d in the Cutoff facing a raise from the Under-the-Gun player (Claude). 8-5 offsuit is a very weak hand with poor playability and low equity against a strong UTG opening range. With 83.5 big blinds, there is no incentive to speculate or bluff with this trash hand. Folding is the standard and correct decision.

I'm on the button with pocket eights (8c 8s) — a comfortable hand to play aggressively in position. Claude opened UTG to 250; with deep stacks (~144bb) and position advantage I want to take the initiative and try to isolate him rather than just calling and letting the blinds enter cheaply. A standard 3-bet size around 3–3.5x his open is appropriate: large enough to fold out weak speculative hands from the blinds and build a playable pot heads-up, but not so large as to overcommit. I raise to 800 total. Postflop I will c-bet favorable textures and proceed cautiously on heavy overcard boards; if I face a large 4-bet shove I will re-evaluate (likely fold to very large 4-bets, call or shove only versus reasonable sizing).

We’re in the small blind with J5o facing an UTG open and a button 3-bet. This is a dominated, offsuit hand that plays very poorly out of position in a bloated pot, and calling invites the BB in while we’re squeezed between two strong ranges. A 4-bet squeeze bluff is too ambitious here versus UTG strength + a likely value-heavy button 3-bet range, with poor blockers and little postflop playability. Best EV is to cut losses and fold.

I am in the Big Blind holding King-Deuce offsuit. Claude (UTG) opened to 250, and Minni (Button) 3-bet to 800. K2o is a very weak hand to defend against a UTG open and a 3-bet from the button. I am out of position against both players, and the strength shown by a 3-bet over a UTG raise typically indicates a range that dominates my hand. There is no reason to risk chips in this spot.

Facing a 3-bet to 800 from Minni on the button. ATs is too strong to fold here - good pot odds (550 into 1200) and I have a suited broadway hand with nut flush potential. 4-betting OOP seems too aggressive. Calling is standard - I can play postflop with position to act first and can fold to heavy action on bad boards or continue on favorable textures.

Flop missed me - I have ace high with a gutshot straight draw (Q gives me the nut straight). Against Minni's 3-bet range, I'm behind most of the time. Standard play is to check and see what the aggressor does. I have some backdoor heart draw potential and a gutshot. Can call a reasonable c-bet or fold to heavy pressure.

I 3-bet preflop and am in position with pocket eights on a fairly dry J♦ 2♣ 9♥ flop. Claude called my 3-bet from UTG, so his range has some made hands (Jx, 99, 22, big pairs) but also a lot of broadways and A‑highs that missed. With a medium pocket pair I have decent showdown value and good reason to take the initiative: a small-to-medium c-bet can fold out overcards and unpaired broadways while keeping the pot manageable against the hands that currently beat me. I’ll bet ~40% pot (700 chips) — large enough to apply pressure and deny free cards, but small enough given deep stacks so I can continue on a blank turn or fold to significant resistance. If he folds I win the pot; if he calls I’ll reassess on the turn and proceed cautiously versus signs of strength (raise/shove).

I have A-high with a gutshot straight draw (8 gives me 8-9-T-J). Pot odds are 700 to win 3150, needing ~22% equity. Against Minni's 3-bet range and c-bet, I have A-high (some showdown value), gutshot (4 outs), and backdoor hearts. The c-bet is only 40% pot - not super aggressive. I'll call and reassess on the turn, folding to continued aggression if I don't improve. Folding ATs after calling a 3-bet and seeing a relatively dry board feels too weak.

The Queen on the turn gives me a double gutshot straight draw - I need K (for 9-T-J-Q-K) or 8 (for 8-9-T-J-Q), giving me 8 outs. Minni checked after c-betting the flop, showing weakness or pot control. With position to act and a free card available, I should check back to realize my equity. Betting as a semi-bluff risks getting check-raised, and I can hit my 8-out draw ~17% on the river. Check is the best play here.

The Q on the turn is a fairly bad card for my hand. With 8♣8♠ I'm an underpair to the board and the Q significantly helps a large portion of Claude's calling range (Qx, QJ, Q9, plus some broadway hands that will continue). I opened and c‑bet the flop to take the initiative, but now I don't want to inflate a pot OOP with a hand that has limited fold equity and poor showdown value versus top-pair and improved Q hands. Pot is only ~3,150 and stacks are deep, so I prefer pot control: check and see how Claude reacts on the river. Plan: check here, call a small-to-medium river bet as a bluff-catcher vs worse, and fold to a large shove or polarizing raise.

River: J-2-9-Q-9. My straight draw missed (needed K or 8). I have A-high with no showdown value against most of Minni's 3-bet range. Minni checked the turn showing weakness. My options: 1) Check and potentially win if Minni also has a missed draw like AK, or 2) Bluff to fold out weak pairs. Given my A-high has some showdown value against busted draws and bluffing would be risky against a player who 3-bet pre, checking is the prudent play. If Minni bets, I fold. If they check behind, I might win at showdown occasionally.

I 3‑bet preflop with 8♣8♠ and c‑bet the J♦2♣9♥ flop; Claude (UTG) called, then both of us checked the Q♠ turn and he checked the river 9♣. On this river my eights are an underpair to both J and Q and the board pairing the 9 greatly helps a lot of hands in Claude’s continuing range (Jx, Qx, 9x, sets). Claude’s line (call flop, check turn, check river) is consistent with either a cautious made hand or a trap, and there’s limited fold equity — a bet will mainly fold A/K high hands I already beat and will get called by the hands that beat me. With deep stacks and a relatively small pot I’m not willing to bluff into a caller-heavy range or risk a check‑raise on the river. I’ll exercise pot control and take this to showdown by checking.

UTG in a 6-handed game with Q9o offsuit is a standard fold. This hand plays poorly out of position and lacks the strength to open-raise or limp profitably against the likely calling/raising ranges from later positions. Opponents have deep stacks, but folding preserves chips for premium spots early in the tournament (hand 35/150). No strong reads from history justify playing this marginal hand first in.

I am in Middle Position with King-Seven offsuit (K7o) after the UTG player folded. This is a weak hand that falls outside the standard opening range for this position, as it is easily dominated by hands like KQ, KJ, KT, or A-K, and plays poorly post-flop out of position against the remaining opponents (Cutoff, Button, Blinds). With aggressive players like Minni yet to act, opening this hand carries negative expected value. Therefore, the best course of action is to fold.

Cutoff with A♥T♣ (Ah Tc) — a clear open-raise candidate. Pot is only 150 and to call 100 (limping) surrenders initiative and invites multiway action; folding is too tight. I’ll open to 300 (≈3x BB) — a standard size at this table that gets fold equity against the button and blinds, keeps my range strong, and gives me position postflop. If I get single-raised by a tight player I’ll give up, but I’ll call or play back against lighter 3-bets depending on sizing and stack-to-pot. If called, I’ll continue to c-bet favorable flops (A/T/X, dry boards) and control the pot on dangerous textures.

Button vs cutoff open, AJs suited is a strong candidate to 3-bet for value and initiative. Minni has been opening frequently, so we gain fold equity while also getting called by worse broadways/medium pairs. In position, a ~3x 3-bet size pressures their range without bloating to an awkward SPR. If faced with a large 4-bet, we can comfortably fold and preserve stack; if called, we play a strong, nut-potential hand in position.

I am in the Small Blind with Jack-Deuce offsuit (Js 2c), which is a very weak hand. The action ahead of me involves an open raise to 300 from Minni (Cutoff) and a 3-bet to 900 from FiveTwo (Button). Given my poor position and the strength shown by the opponents' actions, there is no reason to continue with this hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

T7 suited is a marginal hand facing a 3-bet. Minni opened to 300 and FiveTwo 3-bet to 900. I need to call 800 to continue. T7s doesn't play well against 3-bet ranges - I'll often be dominated or have poor equity. Even with implied odds, this is too loose of a call out of position against a 3-bet with another player still to act behind. Fold is the disciplined play here.

I opened CO to 300 with A♥T♣ and the button (FiveTwo) 3-bet to 900 — a fairly small sizing that often represents a wide, sometimes light 3-bet from the button. I’m now out of position postflop, so flatting is preferred to a light 4-bet with ATo: a large 4-bet is too committal and a tiny 4-bet buys little fold equity. Calling 600 more (900 total committed) gives ~3.25:1 pot odds (~23% equity needed) and keeps worse Aces, broadways and weaker hands in his range that I can play against. Plan: see the flop and c-bet on A-high or dry boards, proceed cautiously on coordinated/wet textures, and fold to heavy continued aggression if I don’t improve. Call 900.

I called the button’s light 3-bet preflop and am out of position on a J♦-5♦-3♠ flop with A♥T♣. I have only ace-high and no diamond backdoors, so this is not an ideal c-bet spot: the board gives villain lots of Jx and diamond-draw combos that connect well with a 3-better’s range. Betting OOP would build a pot with a marginal hand and lose fold equity against hands that will continue; checking controls the pot and lets me realize equity and proceed based on villain’s action. Plan: check. If villain checks back I get a free turn to barrel profitably on A/T runouts; if villain fires a small bet (roughly ≤1/3 pot ≈650) I’ll call to keep worse aces/broadways and pursue my outs; if villain bets medium/large (≥1/2 pot) or raises, I’ll give up most of the time and fold to heavy aggression — no check-raise bluff here given my lack of a diamond or pair.

We 3-bet pre and get a favorable flop for our range. With AcJc we have top pair/top kicker in a 3-bet pot (SPR ~3.4), so we should c-bet for value and protection versus Minni’s likely continuing range (worse Jx like QJ/JT, pocket pairs like TT-88, and diamond draws/A5s-type hands). A ~1/3 pot sizing keeps dominated hands in, applies pressure to their overcards, and charges the flush draw without over-inflating the pot into a spot where we face a costly check-raise from sets/strong Jx. Plan: comfortably continue versus a reasonable check-raise (often call) and keep barreling many safe turns.

I opened CO with A♥T♣ and flat-called the button’s small 3‑bet preflop. Flop J♦‑5♦‑3♠ is unfriendly to my hand — I only have ace‑high and no diamond backdoor — but the button’s bet (650 into a 2600 pot, a very small sizing) is consistent with a wide continuation range and many bluffs/probes. Raising OOP with just Ace‑high is too thin and commits me unnecessarily. Calling keeps worse A‑x and broadways in and gives me a chance to improve. I have 6 clean outs (three Aces, three Tens); my two‑card equity to pair by the river is ≈27.4%, while calling 650 to win a ~3250 pot requires ≈20% equity, so calling is +EV. Plan: call now, see the turn — if I pair an A or T I will value bet; if the turn brings a dangerous card or villain shows heavy aggression (a raise or a large bet) I will fold.

I have A♥T♣ and missed the turn (board 3♠ J♦ 5♦ 9♠). I called a light 3‑bet preflop and a small flop bet, so my range looks reasonably strong, but I currently only have ace‑high with no diamond backdoor. I have 6 clean outs (three Aces, three Tens) — ~6/46 ≈ 13% to improve on the river — which is too little to justify barreling out of position into a ~3.25k pot. Betting OOP here would bloat the pot with a marginal hand and has poor fold equity against a button’s continuing range (many Jx, diamond draws, and worse Aces/broads that will call). Checking controls the pot and preserves the chance to see a free river where I can barrel/lead if I pair, or fold to aggression if I miss. Plan: check turn. If villain bets into the river I’ll reassess based on sizing (fold to large/forcible bets; consider calling small probes given pot odds or my river improvement).

We have TPTK (AcJc) in a 3-bet pot after c-betting flop and getting called. Minni’s flop call range contains plenty of worse made hands (Jx like QJ/JT, TT–88, 5x) and a lot of diamond/straight draws that picked up or retained equity on the 9s turn. With a draw-heavy board and still a clear range/position advantage, we should keep betting for value and to deny equity, while sizing up from the flop to set up a clean river shove on many runouts. A ~55% pot bet accomplishes this without overpolarizing into only sets.

I have A♥T♣ and currently only ace‑high — no diamond backdoor and I missed the 9♠ turn. After calling a light 3‑bet and a small flop C‑bet my line looks weak; FiveTwo’s turn bet of 1,800 into a 5,050 pot is asking for a real commitment. I have 6 clean outs (3 A + 3 T) ≈13% to improve on the river, but the pot odds required to call are on the order of ~20–26% (depending on exact pot accounting), so a call is -EV. A bluff‑raise OOP is too thin here — villain’s preflop 3‑bet + continued aggression suggests enough hands that will call or shove that my fold equity is limited. I’ll preserve my healthy stack and fold.

I am in UTG position with Ten of Hearts and Three of Hearts (T3s). This is a very weak starting hand, rated poorly in almost all pre-flop charts, especially from early position. With 5 players left to act behind me, the likelihood of someone waking up with a better hand is high, and playing this hand out of position is difficult and unprofitable. My stack is healthy at 83.5 BB, so there is no pressure to make desperate moves. The standard and correct play is to fold.

I’m dealt Q♦4♦ in middle position with deep stacks (~145 BB). This is a marginal, easily-dominated hand with poor postflop playability; limp-calling invites multiway pots and raises, and open-raising is thin given aggressive players behind (Cutoff/Button/SB). With lots of hands left and no compelling reason to invest chips here, I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

Folds to me in the cutoff with K9o in a 6-handed game. This is comfortably within a profitable CO opening range: we have decent high-card strength, a king blocker, and position on the blinds with the button still to act. Best play is to raise (not limp) to apply pressure and pick up the blinds/deny equity. I’ll use a standard, risk-controlled 2.5x open and be prepared to fold to a strong 3-bet from the more aggressive players.

I am on the button with 8h 3s, which is an extremely weak hand. FiveTwo has raised from the cutoff to 250. There is no justification for playing this hand profitably against a raise, even with positional advantage. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

A5o in the small blind facing a cutoff open to 2.5x. This is a marginal spot - I have an ace blocker but the hand plays poorly OOP. Folding is fine but at only 200 more to call into a 400 pot, I'm getting 2:1 odds. The BB could squeeze which is a concern. I'll flat call here to see a flop cheaply - folding is also reasonable but A-high has some showdown potential and occasional wheel/flush draws.

QTo is a solid defensive hand in the BB against a cutoff raise, even with the SB call. Positionally OOP multiway, but pot odds are excellent (150 to call into 600 pot, ~4:1). QTo has good playability with straight potential and blocks Q/K. FiveTwo raises frequently from late position (e.g., Hands 7,14,26,35), suggesting a wide range we dominate parts of. Claude's SB call is likely wide/speculative. Flatting keeps pot multiway, allows postflop control, and avoids bloating pot OOP with a marginal 3bet. Folding too tight early in tournament with deep stacks.

I have A5o on J64hh - just ace high with a gutshot (need a 3 for wheel). Two hearts on board means flush draw possible. I'm OOP against the preflop raiser (FiveTwo) and Elon.
